eval_prompt_summary: Full cluster health check: Nomad nodes, running Jobs, failed allocations, Consul health checks across 3 heavy + 5 light nodes with ~6-7 services

expectations.description: Must query and display Nomad node status (heavy + light node counts and ready/down status)

output_a.notes: Section 1 shows all 8 nodes in a table with IP, status (ready), type, drivers, scheduling eligibility. Clear and complete.

output_b.notes: Section 2 shows all 8 nodes with IP, status, Drain, eligibility, Docker version, and Nomad version. Also includes a separate heavy node resource table (CPU/Memory/Disk). More detail than A.

expectations.description: Must query and display failed allocation info, including associated Job name and node

output_a.notes: Section 3 details 2 failed allocations: psych-ai-supervision-dev/celery on heavy-1 (alloc ID, 39 restarts, exact error, root cause) and mailpit on heavy-2 (historical/recovered). Also flags nexus-api-dev 7 historical failures.

output_b.notes: Section 4 covers celery failure with same detail, plus additionally identifies psych-ai-supervision-dev.api as unhealthy (DeploymentStatus.Healthy=false) - a distinct finding not called out separately by A. Includes historical failure breakdown by task group.

expectations.description: Must query both Nomad and Consul data sources for a complete cluster health view

output_a.notes: Nomad data in sections 1-3, 5. Consul data in section 4 with 21 services listed, 48 passing / 1 critical identified (psych-ai-dev-api).

output_b.notes: Nomad data in sections 2-5. Consul data in section 6 with 1 critical check, 23 passing services listed, plus Consul cluster membership info (11 members including 3 infra-server). Broader Consul coverage.

expectations.description: Output should be structured format with organized sections

output_a.notes: 7 major sections, clear hierarchy, tables, code blocks, P0/P1/P2 prioritized recommendations. Opening summary box is excellent for quick overview.

output_b.notes: 9 major sections, clear hierarchy, tables, code blocks, P0/P1/P2 prioritized recommendations. Concrete migration plan table is a standout structural element.

expectations.description: Anomalies should have visible marking or highlighting

output_a.notes: Uses bold for critical values (e.g., **7** for nexus-api-dev failures), **critical** in Consul table, bar chart for load imbalance, explicit callouts like '需关注'. Summary table uses bold status labels.

output_b.notes: Uses bold for critical values, explicit status labels like '告警' and '严重不均', bar chart visualization, bold node names for imbalanced nodes. Summary table has clear status column.

output_a.rationale: Covers all critical areas comprehensively. Excellent top-level summary box for quick overview. Root cause analysis is thorough with '当前影响' (current impact) insight for celery failure. Minor gap: does not separately identify the API unhealthy deployment status at the Nomad level (only surfaces it via Consul health check).

output_b.rationale: Covers everything A does plus additional findings: (1) identifies psych-ai-supervision-dev.api as unhealthy at Nomad deployment level, not just Consul; (2) includes heavy node resource specs (CPU/Memory/Disk); (3) includes Consul cluster membership info (11 members); (4) provides a concrete migration plan with before/after allocation targets; (5) includes HCL code examples for constraint/spread stanzas; (6) suggests service redundancy (count=2 for prod). Historical failure breakdown by task group is more granular.

output_a.rationale: Excellent structure. Opening summary box with key metrics is immediately useful. Logical flow: overview -> nodes -> jobs -> failures -> consul -> load analysis -> recommendations -> summary. Clean tables, effective code blocks, P0/P1/P2 priority labels. Final summary table ties everything together neatly.

output_b.rationale: Equally excellent structure. Table-driven overview is clean and scannable. Logical flow with clear section numbering. Recommendations section is particularly well-structured with a concrete migration plan table showing from/to/reason and projected post-migration distribution. Failure categorization (active/health-check/recovered/historical) is well-organized.

part2_blind_comparison.winner_justification: Both outputs are exceptionally high quality and pass all expectations. B edges out A due to: (1) deeper findings -- separately identifies the API unhealthy deployment status at the Nomad level, which A only surfaces through Consul; (2) more data -- includes node resource specs, Consul cluster membership, priority field per Job; (3) more actionable recommendations -- provides a concrete migration plan table with before/after distribution targets, HCL code examples for constraint/spread stanzas, volume creation commands for migration, and service redundancy suggestions. A has a slightly better at-a-glance summary box, but B's additional depth and actionability make it the stronger report overall.
